source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Subsea Tree Valve Leak
actions_taken: ROV manually closed CIT1 valve with five clockwise turns
SST Supervisor hydraulically functioned CIT1 valve open and closed
Pressure tested CIT1 valve to 3500 psi
Shell initiated procedure changes to verify subsea tree valve status
event.summary: On 18 March 2022 at 1800 hours, during abandonment operations on Shell's Alaminos Canyon 815 well SA001 from the Helix Q-4000, a pressure test of a five-barrel cement squeeze failed to hold 1000 psi. Investigation revealed the chemical injection tree valve 1 (CIT1) on the subsea tree was leaking approximately 188 barrels of a 50/50 mixture of Mono Ethylene Glycol and seawater into the Gulf of Mexico. The CIT1 valve had not been functioned to verify closure prior to the pressure test; operators relied on documented position from well handover procedures. Manual ROV intervention and hydraulic functioning of the valve eventually stopped the leak.
recommendations: Shell to modify Shell Perdido SA001 Well Handover Procedure to include procedures for verifying status of all valves on the subsea tree

hazards.name: Mono Ethylene Glycol and seawater mixture

threats.name: CIT1 valve not fully closed prior to pressure test
Failure to verify valve position before operation
Reliance on historical valve position documentation without functional verification

consequences.name: Discharge of 188 barrels of MEG/seawater mixture to Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Pressure test procedure per 30 CFR 250.1715
controls.barrier_role: Detect failed cement squeeze and valve leakage

supporting_text: The pressure test failed to hold 1000 psi over 15 minutes as required per 30 CFR 250.1715

controls.name: Well Handover Procedure documentation
controls.barrier_role: Verify valve status prior to operations

supporting_text: The CIT1 valve was documented and verified as closed on the Shell Perdido SA001 Well Handover Procedure by the Well Site supervisor
Shell used the last documented position of CIT1 valve as a verification of the valve being in the closed position
The exact position of the CIT1 valve was not known since the CIT1 valve was never functioned before or during the operation

controls.name: CIT1 valve mechanical position indicator
controls.barrier_role: Visual indication of valve closure status

supporting_text: The CIT1 mechanical position indicator, visually appeared to be in the closed position but the CIT1 valve was not fully closed
The CIT1 position indicator moved slightly when the ROV manually functioned CIT1 valve

controls.name: Subsea Tree Supervisor hydraulic valve functioning
controls.barrier_role: Restore valve to closed position and stop leak

supporting_text: The TechnipFMC Subsea Tree (SST) Supervisor hydraulically functioned open and close the CIT1 valve three times with no movement showing on the CIT1 mechanical position indicator
The CIT1 valve was then hydraulically functioned open and closed by the SST Supervisor before the leak stopped

controls.name: ROV manual valve closure intervention
controls.barrier_role: Manually close CIT1 valve to stop leak

supporting_text: The SST Supervisor then attempted to use the ROV to manually close the CIT1 with five clockwise turns
The manual attempt resulted in slight movement on the CIT1 mechanical position indicator

controls.name: Post-incident pressure test of CIT1 valve
controls.barrier_role: Verify valve integrity and proper function after intervention

supporting_text: The CIT1 valve was successfully pressure tested to 3500 psi, and the CIT1 valve worked as designed for the rest of the abandonment operation

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
